commit.message:
Overhaul code runner: Pyodide for Python, Godbolt for compiled, native for HTML/JS

Python:
- Runs entirely in the browser via Pyodide (WASM) loaded from CDN
- Persistent hidden iframe — Pyodide loads once (~10 MB), reused for all runs
- Auto-installs imported packages via loadPackagesFromImports (numpy, pandas,
  scipy, matplotlib, sympy, sklearn, PIL, networkx, cryptography, pydantic, ...)
- Matplotlib plots captured as PNG and displayed as images in the panel
- 60 s timeout

HTML/SVG:
- Rendered in sandboxed iframe with allow-scripts + allow-pointer-lock
- Three.js, Babylon.js, WebGL, canvas games all work via CDN script tags

JavaScript/JSX:
- Sandboxed iframe with console.log/warn/error capture via postMessage
- 5 s timeout

Compiled languages (via Godbolt Compiler Explorer — free, no auth, CORS OK):
- C++ → GCC 15.2 (g152)
- C   → GCC 15.2 (cg152)
- Go  → gc 1.26.0 (gl1260)
- Rust → rustc 1.94.0 (r1940)
- Java → JDK 25.0.1 (java2501)
- Kotlin → kotlinc 2.2.20
- Swift → swiftc 6.2
- Haskell → GHC 9.12.2
- Ruby → 3.4.7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: don't attach to unmanaged copilot servers on the default port

ensureCopilotServer() was blindly trusting any process listening on
port 4321, including stale manually-started servers from weeks ago
that don't support per-session MCP or instructions.

Now only attaches to servers started by otto's own bgtask. If an
unmanaged process is on the port, starts on an alternate port
instead. User-managed servers (via dashboard.copilot_server config)
bypass this entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: encrypt all metadata at rest via AES-256-GCM with HKDF per-file keys (#1203)

EncryptedFileIO: thin AES-256-GCM utility deriving per-file keys from
BMW_WAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY via HKDF-SHA256. Wire format: ENCF magic + nonce
+ ciphertext + tag. Backward compatible — detects plaintext files by
missing magic and passes through unchanged.

Encrypted storage points:
- WalSnapshot: snapshot files (keys + head pointers)
- WalTableKeyAllocator: sequence ID files
- WalDataProvider: ID maps (per entity) and schema JSON files
- SearchIndexManager: search index binary files
- DiskBufferedLogger: audit log lines (per-line encryption)

Not yet covered: LocalPagedFile data pages (needs page format redesign
for per-page nonce+tag overhead — tracked separately).

Closes #1203

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix PostgreSQL transaction abort on concurrent lock initialization in clustered mode (#2829)

* Initial plan

* Add PostgreSQLRowLockSemaphore to fix transaction abort issue

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix build errors in PostgreSQLRowLockSemaphore

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix test and verify PostgreSQL lock race condition is resolved

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback - refactor SQL construction and add delay comment

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Final fixes: use const string for SQL and fix test delay placement

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix critical logic bug in PostgreSQL lock semaphore

- Override ExecuteSQL to handle ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING returning 0 rows
- When res == 0, retry SELECT...FOR UPDATE to acquire the lock
- Use proper constants (TablePrefixSubst, TableLocks, ColumnSchedulerName, ColumnLockName)
- Consolidate duplicate test cases into single test
- Remove unnecessary Assert.Pass() statements

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix critical correctness bug: throw when res==0 on final retry

When INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING returns res==0 on the final retry,
we must throw an exception instead of falling through to return success.
Otherwise, the code incorrectly reports lock acquisition success even
though SELECT...FOR UPDATE never succeeded, breaking mutual exclusion.

Added terminal throw for res==0 branch when retries are exhausted,
mirroring the res!=1 branch behavior.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unnecessary ExecuteSQL override to fix SonarQube quality gate

The base class StdRowLockSemaphore.ExecuteSQL already handles the
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING case correctly:
- When res == 0 (conflict), the if (res != 1) check at line 128 is true
- It delays and continues, looping back to SELECT...FOR UPDATE
- The second SELECT finds the row and acquires the lock

The ExecuteSQL override was duplicating base class logic (29.9% duplication)
and is unnecessary. Removing it fixes the SonarQube quality gate failure
while maintaining the same correct behavior.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix test to properly exercise INSERT race condition

Replaced scheduler.Clear() with direct DELETE FROM QRTZ_LOCKS to ensure
the locks table is empty before the test runs. The Clear() call was
acquiring the TRIGGER_ACCESS lock which would pre-populate the locks
table, preventing the test from exercising the INSERT...ON CONFLICT
race condition that this PR fixes.

Now the test definitively covers the concurrent INSERT scenario where
multiple threads race to create the same lock row.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log lock handler type name after instantiation

Moved logging after lock handler instantiation and now logs the actual
type name (without namespace) for both db-based and thread monitor-based
locking paths. This provides better visibility into which lock handler
implementation is being used (e.g., PostgreSQLRowLockSemaphore vs
StdRowLockSemaphore vs SimpleSemaphore).

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: guard SVE intrinsics with conditional compilation to prevent x64 segfault

NativeTernaryMatrix: wrap DotProductSve, DotProductSve2, SVE static
fields, InitSveDecodePatterns, and Sve.IsSupported checks with
#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER / #pragma warning disable SYSLIB5003 guards.

Without these guards, AOT/JIT compiles ARM SVE instructions that cause
SIGILL/segfault on x64. Runtime Sve.IsSupported dispatch checks are
insufficient — the method bodies must not be compiled at all on
non-ARM platforms.

Matches the pattern already used correctly in DictionaryColumnFilter.cs
and SimdCapabilities.cs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Finalize block identity and time travel design decisions

BLOCK_IDENTITY.md:
- No opt-out (fundamental to Bloom)
- Eager first-run assignment (10K pages, self-write detection)
- Non-deterministic IDs (stored in file, not regenerated)
- Self-healing on history thread (indexer detects, never blocks)
- Cross-page move detection via persistent notification

TIME_TRAVEL.md:
- SPC H prefix for history (separate from SPC l links)
- Separate git repo in .index/.git/ (no conflict with user's git)
- Prefetch on which-key prefix press (SPC H = free compute time)
- LRU cache with predictive adjacency prefetch
- Reference vault: 10K pages, 10 years, ~8-10 MB git repo
- All interactive operations target < 10ms perceived latency

DAY_VIEW.md / JOURNAL_REDESIGN.md:
- Updated keybindings to SPC H c for calendar

Cosmos CI build optimizations (#48260)

* Optimize Cosmos CI: reduce PR matrix, increase parallelism, skip shade

1. PR-conditional emulator matrix (16 → 11 jobs):
   Drops redundant JDK variants for Spark/Kafka in PR builds.
   Full matrix on main merges.

   Dropped for PRs (5 jobs, ~5 agent hours saved):
   - Spark 3.3 Java 11 (keeping Java 8)
   - Spark 3.4 Java 8 (keeping Java 11)
   - Spark 3.5/Scala 2.12 Java 8 (keeping Java 17)
   - Spark 4.0/Scala 2.13 Java 17 (keeping Java 21)
   - Kafka Java 11 (keeping Java 17)

2. Increase BuildParallelization from 1 to 2 in all stages
   (Build, TestEmulator, TestVNextEmulator).

3. Skip maven-shade-plugin for non-Spark/non-Kafka emulator jobs:
   Core emulator, long emulator, and encryption jobs don't need
   Spark/Kafka uber JARs. Adding -Dshade.skip=true saves ~90s of
   shade plugin execution per Spark module × 5 modules = ~7-8 min
   per non-Spark job (5 jobs × 7 min = ~35 min agent time saved).

4. Remove outdated comment about emulator download time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: also skip maven-antrun-plugin when shade is skipped

The antrun 03-repack phase expects shade output (native .jnilib/.so
files in target/tmp/). When -Dshade.skip=true, the shade output doesn't
exist and antrun fails with 'Could not find file'. Add
-Dmaven.antrun.skip=true alongside -Dshade.skip=true.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: pass shade.skip to both build AND test steps via AdditionalArgs

The test step runs 'clean verify' which recompiles everything from
scratch, including Spark shade. Our BuildOptions only affected the
build step. Add -Dshade.skip=true -Dmaven.antrun.skip=true to
AdditionalArgs for non-Spark jobs so it flows into TestOptions too.

Keep BuildOptions for the build step as well (both steps need it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BuildOptions plumbing to skip shade in Build stage unit test jobs

Add BuildOptions parameter through ci.yml → ci.tests.yml → build-and-test.yml
pipeline chain. Defaults to empty string (no behavior change for other SDKs).

Cosmos Build stage sets BuildOptions to '-Dshade.skip=true -Dmaven.antrun.skip=true'
to skip Spark/Kafka uber JAR creation during unit test matrix jobs, saving ~14 min
per job. The release artifact deploy step is unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add per-job ProjectListOverride for Spark/Kafka emulator jobs

Each Spark emulator job previously compiled ALL 14 modules including
other Spark versions it doesn't test, wasting ~11 min per job on
unnecessary shade+compile.

Changes:
- generate-project-list.ps1: Check for ProjectListOverride env var
  at the top. If set, use it directly and skip normal computation.
  Defaults to empty (no behavior change for other SDKs).
- Emulator matrix JSONs: Add ProjectListOverride for each Spark and
  Kafka job with only the modules they need (core + their specific
  Spark/Kafka module).

Example: Spark 3.5/2.13 job previously built 14 modules (41 min test
step). Now builds only 6 modules, saving ~11 min per Spark job.

Estimated savings: ~11 min × 9 Spark jobs + ~5 min × 2 Kafka jobs
= ~109 min agent time per full CI run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* No-op: trigger Cosmos CI for build optimization validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ProjectListOverride for emulator/encryption jobs to skip Spark compilation

Emulator, Long Emulator, and Encryption jobs were compiling all 14 cosmos
modules including 7 Spark modules (Scala compilation ~10-16 min) despite
only running core emulator tests. Add ProjectListOverride to limit these
jobs to only the modules they actually test:
- Emulator/Long Emulator: azure-cosmos, azure-cosmos-test, azure-cosmos-tests
- Encryption: adds azure-cosmos-encryption

Also reverts the no-op TestSuiteBase trigger commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard ProjectListOverride from being used in FromSource runs

FromSource runs use ClientFromSourcePom.xml to build all track 2 libraries
and rely on the computed project list for -pl/-amd test scoping. Allowing
ProjectListOverride to take effect during FromSource runs would restrict
the test scope incorrectly. Check $env:TESTFROMSOURCE and skip the
override when it is 'true'.
